title: Economy (world level)
description: Trade routes and commodity baselines for a world. Used to inject prices and route status into shop/trade scenes and to react to active tropes (e.g. 'bandit_blockade' marks a route as disrupted, doubling grain prices on it). PRD §2.1. Lives at sidequest-content/genre_packs/<pack>/worlds/<world>/economy.yaml.

trade_routes.description: Optional. Inter-settlement trade arcs. Each route is directional in name only — `from` and `to` describe the typical flow, but goods move both ways via `return_goods`.

commodity_baseline.description: Map of commodity id (e.g. 'iron_ore', 'tea', 'fuel_cells') to baseline economics. Commodity ids are open enum — genre-typed.

currency.description: Optional. In-world currency name + base unit. If omitted, prices in `commodity_baseline.base_price` are abstract scalars the narrator translates contextually.

from.description: Origin settlement name. SHOULD match a settlement in demographics.yaml.

to.description: Destination settlement name. SHOULD match a settlement in demographics.yaml.

goods.description: Commodities moved from `from` to `to`. Ids reference `commodity_baseline`.

return_goods.description: Optional. Commodities moved from `to` back to `from`.

danger.description: Open enum: 'safe', 'moderate', 'dangerous', or genre-specific. Narrator references when describing travel.

status.description: Current route status. Closed enum — the trope engine may mutate this at runtime.

base_price.description: Price in the world's currency for one unit (unit is genre-implicit; tea may be 'a pot', iron may be 'a bar').

abundance.description: Optional. Open enum: 'rare', 'low', 'moderate', 'high', 'glut'. Drives price modifiers when supply state changes.

source.description: Optional. Free-form prose explaining the origin (e.g. 'Grimholt mines', 'Glenross gardens, mostly Mrs. Pettigrew's').
